SEARCH
Package edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.list

Class ArrayBasedList<E>

java.lang.Object
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.list.AbstractList<E>
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.list.ArrayBasedList<E>
Type Parameters:
E - the type of elements stored in the list
All Implemented Interfaces:
List<E>, Iterable<E>
public class ArrayBasedList<E> extends AbstractList<E>
An array-based list is a contiguous-memory representation of the List abstract data type. This array-based list dynamically resizes to ensure O(1) amortized cost for adding to the end of the list. Size is maintained as a global field to allow for O(1) size() and isEmpty() behaviors.
Author:
Dr. King, Diya Patel

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ArrayBasedList()
    Constructs a new instance of an array-based list data structure with the default initial capacity of the internal array
    ArrayBasedList(int capacity)
    Constructs a new instance of an array-based list data structure with the provided initial capacity

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(int index, E element)
    The code for this method is based on the mergesort algorithm on page 260 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser.

  • Constructor Details

    • ArrayBasedList

      public ArrayBasedList()
      Constructs a new instance of an array-based list data structure with the default initial capacity of the internal array

    • ArrayBasedList

      public ArrayBasedList(int capacity)
      Constructs a new instance of an array-based list data structure with the provided initial capacity
      Parameters:
      capacity - the initial capacity of the internal array used to store the list elements

  • Method Details

    • add

      public void add(int index, E element)
      The code for this method is based on the mergesort algorithm on page 260 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser. Adds a new element to the list at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - the index at which to add the new element to the list
      element - the new element to add to the list
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the provided index is not a valid index based on the current state of the list

    • get

      public E get(int index)
      Returns the element at the specified index of the list
      Parameters:
      index - the index at which to retrieve the element
      Returns:
      the element at the specified index of the list
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the specified index is not a valid index based on the current state of the list

    • remove

      public E remove(int index)
      The code for this method is based on the mergesort algorithm on page 260 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser. Removes and returns the element at the specified index of the list
      Parameters:
      index - the index of the element to remove from the list
      Returns:
      rtn the element at the specified index of the list
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the provided index is not a valid index based on the current state of the list

    • set

      public E set(int index, E element)
      The code for this method is based on the mergesort algorithm on page 260 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser. Updates the element at the specified index of the list
      Parameters:
      index - the index at which an existing element should be updated
      element - the new element to store are the provided index
      Returns:
      rtn the original element that was replaced by the updated element
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the provided index is not a valid index based on the current state of the list

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of elements in the list
      Returns:
      the number of elements in the list

    • iterator

      public Iterator<E> iterator()
      interator for the arraybased List
      Returns:
      a new instance of the element iterator
